name: charity: water
mission: charity: water is a nonprofit organization bringing clean and safe drinking water to people in developing countries. The organization inspires giving and empowers others to fundraise for sustainable water solutions. 100% of public donations are sent to local partners on the ground, who build and implement the projects. When the projects are completed, charity: water proves every one of them using GPS coordinates, photos and details of the community served.
theoryOfChange: Charity Water utilizes a 'MAP Framework' which is a monitoring and evaluation framework designed to collect data across various water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) indicators. This framework helps them understand the impact of funded projects, maximize effectiveness, and guide future funding decisions. The MAP Framework includes two types of data collection: Annual Check-ins (household surveys at baseline and endline of a grant) and Post-Implementation Monitoring (a comprehensive assessment of past work every few years). The framework aims to answer questions about immediate improvements, sustained access, and reasons for water point functionality issues.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The global water crisis requires billions in investment; the charity's <cite id="18">2.1% revenue CAGR</cite> shows steady but absorbable growth.

strengths.detail: The organization holds a <cite id="4">perfect 100.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="7">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
They spend only <cite id="6">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.
The charity has a documented track record of <cite id="3">funding 9,395 water projects</cite> and utilizes GPS tracking for project transparency.

strengths_deep_dive: charity: water distinguishes itself through a rigorous commitment to financial health, evidenced by <cite id="8">10.1 months of working capital</cite> available to sustain operations. This resilience is paired with a high governance standard, featuring a <cite id="9">board of 12 members</cite> with <cite id="10">91% independent membership</cite>. Their operational model is highly effective in the extreme poverty sector, achieving an estimated cost of <cite id="11">$3.17 per beneficiary</cite>, which is considered exceptional for water and sanitation interventions.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2006, charity: water is a global non-profit dedicated to solving the water crisis by providing <cite id="1">clean and safe drinking water</cite> to communities in need. The organization utilizes a diverse array of technical solutions, including <cite id="2">drilled wells, spring protections, and piped systems</cite>, tailored to the specific needs of the regions they serve. With a focus on sustainability and community-led maintenance, they have successfully <cite id="3">funded 9,395 water projects</cite> as of their most recent reporting period. Financially, the organization is characterized by exceptional transparency, maintaining a <cite id="4">perfect 100/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite>. In 2022, the charity reported <cite id="5">total revenue of $96,818,300</cite>, demonstrating significant scale and the ability to manage large-scale humanitarian operations. Their unique '100% model' ensures that public donations go directly to water projects, while private donors and sponsors cover all operating costs, contributing to a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite>.
